sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Alibaba Group (NYSE: BABA) files 6-K with February 2026 HK Monthly Return
feed.summary: Alibaba Group (NYSE: BABA) has filed a Form 6-K with the U.S. SEC to furnish its Hong Kong Stock Exchange monthly return for February 2026. This filing is a regulatory requirement for Hong Kong–listed issuers under Rule 13.25B of the Hong Kong Listing Rules, reporting movements in authorized share capital and issued shares. The document details the company's capital structure, including share option schemes, convertible senior notes, and restricted share units, with confirmations of compliance to listing rules.

feed.title: WEYS: Earnings and sales fell amid tariff-driven margin pressure, with Florsheim outperforming peers
feed.summary: Weyco Group (WEYS) reported a decline in net sales and earnings year-over-year. This downturn was attributed to lower demand and significant tariff impacts, which compressed gross margins. While most major brands saw sales drops, Florsheim was an exception, showing resilience.

feed.title: gcm-20241231
feed.summary: This Form 10-K filing for GCM Grosvenor Inc. details the company's financial performance and operational activities for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024. The report highlights an increase in total operating revenues to $514.0 million, driven by growth in both management and incentive fees, and provides comprehensive financial statements, non-GAAP financial measures, and disclosures on market risks and regulatory compliance. It also outlines the company's strategies, including expanding client relationships, global footprint, and distribution channels, while addressing risks related to competition, cybersecurity, and regulatory scrutiny.

feed.title: News | Retail off-price giants Ross, TJ Maxx parent ring up more store openings
feed.summary: Off-price retailers like Ross Dress for Less and TJX Cos. are significantly expanding their store footprints in 2026, driven by an increased demand for bargains from cost-conscious consumers. Ross plans to open 110 new stores, including 85 Ross Dress for Less and 25 dd’s Discounts locations, while TJX Cos. (parent to T.J. Maxx, Marshalls, HomeGoods) aims to add 146 new stores globally. This growth is facilitated by an easier real estate market due to retail bankruptcies and the strong consumer draw towards the "treasure hunt" shopping experience these stores offer.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials downgraded at J.P. Morgan after Q4 results missed expectations (VMC:NYSE)
feed.summary: J.P. Morgan downgraded Vulcan Materials (VMC) to Neutral from Overweight, reducing its price target from $335 to $320. This downgrade follows the company's Q4 results and FY 2026 guidance falling short of expectations. The construction materials producer's stock was down 1.3% in Wednesday's trading.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Digital Realty Trust (DLR) After Its Strong Multi Year Run?
feed.summary: This article evaluates Digital Realty Trust (DLR) following its recent strong performance, questioning if it still offers good value. It uses Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis, which suggests DLR is undervalued by 24.8%, and Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio comparison, indicating it's overvalued compared to its industry and peers. The analysis also presents bull and bear case narratives, with a bull case fair value of US$197.78 and a bear case of US$110.45 per share, encouraging investors to align with the narrative that best fits their view of the company's future.

feed.title: Copart Weak Quarter And DOJ Probe Test Long Term Growth Story
feed.summary: Copart, Inc. reported a weak quarter with revenue and earnings missing expectations, and its stock has underperformed peers. The company is also facing a DOJ investigation, adding regulatory risk to its operational challenges. Investors are focused on how Copart will address the DOJ probe, manage current operational issues, and whether its global expansion strategies can mitigate these headwinds.

feed.title: Airlines: Fuel Price Spike Could Mar Spring Results
feed.summary: A recent 12% surge in crude oil prices, triggered by geopolitical events, could significantly impact airline profitability. Fuel costs, which typically account for 20-25% of airlines' unit costs, were not anticipated at these current levels when tickets were sold in advance. This unexpected increase may erase potential profits for airlines in March, and if sustained, could lead to higher ticket prices and reduced leisure travel demand.

feed.title: [144] Expedia Group, Inc. SEC Filing
feed.summary: This SEC filing is a Form 144 Notice of Proposed Sale of Securities for Expedia Group, Inc. (EXPE). It details a proposed sale of 8,225 shares of Expedia Common stock with an aggregate market value of $1,815,891.63 by Nate Skow of Merrill, acquired through RSUs on February 15, 2024. The filing indicates a neutral impact and sentiment according to Rhea-AI.

feed.title: A. O. Smith (AOS) SVP Schuh sells 1,104 shares in open market
feed.summary: A. O. Smith Corporation's SVP, Darrell W. Schuh, sold 1,104 shares of common stock in an open market transaction at a weighted average price of $74.3898 per share. Following this sale, Schuh directly holds 2,201 shares of AOS. The transaction was disclosed via an SEC Form 4 filing and also noted his participation in the company's Dividend Reinvestment Plan.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Unveils Major Plan to Protect Costa Maya’s Fragile Coastline
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean has announced a significant multi-year plan to combat sargassum seaweed blooms along the coast of Mahahual, Quintana Roo, Mexico, which is the site of their upcoming Perfect Day Mexico destination. This initiative involves deploying new equipment, strengthening barriers, and collaborating with local authorities and communities to manage sargassum not just around their private destination but across the broader southern Quintana Roo coastline. The program aims to protect the fragile marine ecosystem, support local livelihoods, and ensure clean beaches for both residents and tourists.

feed.title: Alliant Energy (LNT) director Manu Asthana reports 1,000-share stake
feed.summary: Alliant Energy Corp (LNT) director Manu Asthana has filed an initial ownership report (Form 3) showing a direct holding of 1,000 shares of the company's common stock. This filing indicates current beneficial ownership rather than a recent purchase or sale transaction. The shares are held directly by Mr. Asthana.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Johnson Varkey Sells Shares of Waste Management In
feed.summary: Johnson Varkey, Senior Vice President and Chief Information Officer at Waste Management Inc (WM), sold 617 shares of the company on March 3, 2026. This transaction continues a trend of insider selling, with 34 insider sells against 1 insider buy over the past year. Despite the selling, Waste Management Inc's stock is currently considered fairly valued based on its GF Value, even though its price-earnings ratio is higher than the industry median and its historical median.

feed.title: Sit Advisors report 11.2% stake in Clough Global Equity Fund (GLQ)
feed.summary: Sit Investment Associates, Inc. and Sit Fixed Income Advisors II, LLC reported a shared beneficial ownership of 11.2% in the Clough Global Equity Fund (GLQ), totaling 2,104,530 shares. This disclosure, made via a SCHEDULE 13G/A filing, notes that these shares are held in client accounts, and the filers disclaim direct beneficial ownership but exercise shared voting and dispositive power. The ownership percentage is based on 18,738,120 outstanding shares of GLQ as of October 31, 2025.

feed.title: CMS Energy (NYSE: CMS) officer sells 1,750 shares at $78.21
feed.summary: Scott B. McIntosh, VP, Controller, and Chief Accounting Officer of CMS Energy (NYSE: CMS), sold 1,750 shares of the company's common stock at a price of $78.205 per share. Following this open-market transaction, McIntosh's direct ownership in CMS Energy stands at 24,223 common shares. The reported holdings also include an additional 123 shares acquired through dividend reinvestment or equivalents related to restricted stock awards.

feed.title: Starbucks to open new office in Nashville, move some jobs from Seattle
feed.summary: Starbucks announced plans to open a new corporate office in Nashville, Tennessee, later this year, as part of its broader expansion strategy across North America. The new office will house teams managing the company's supply chain for North America and will offer relocation opportunities to Seattle-based employees, though Seattle will remain the global headquarters. Tennessee Governor Bill Lee welcomed the move, highlighting the state's appealing business environment.

feed.title: A. O. Smith (NYSE: AOS) SVP has 356 shares withheld for taxes
feed.summary: A. O. Smith Corporation's SVP, Darrell W. Schuh, had 356 shares of common stock withheld by the company to cover tax obligations upon the vesting of restricted stock units. This transaction occurred at a price of $79.885 per share. Following this tax-related disposition, Mr. Schuh directly owns 3,295 shares of A. O. Smith common stock.

feed.title: Gilat Satellite Networks Ltd. (NASDAQ:GILT) Sees Significant Decline in Short Interest
feed.summary: Gilat Satellite Networks Ltd. (NASDAQ:GILT) experienced a notable 14.4% decrease in short interest in February, falling to 2,942,970 shares. This decline occurred while several institutional investors significantly increased their stakes in the company, with institutions now owning about 35.72% of the stock. Additionally, Gilat Satellite Networks exceeded quarterly earnings expectations, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $20.00.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Daniel Pope Sells 500 Shares of Tyler Technologies Inc (TYL)
feed.summary: Director Daniel Pope sold 500 shares of Tyler Technologies Inc (TYL) on March 2, 2026, reducing his holding to 2,871 shares. Over the past year, Pope has sold a total of 865 shares, and the company has seen 2 insider buys versus 28 insider sells. Despite the insider selling, TYL stock is currently trading at $355.4, indicating it is significantly undervalued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 0.67, based on a GuruFocus Value of $530.42.

feed.title: HP Inc.: Why This Boring Stock Just Got Seriously Interesting for You
feed.summary: HP Inc. is positioned as more than just a printer manufacturer, evolving into an "AI PC, gaming, and high-dividend cash-flow" investment opportunity. The company is actively participating in the AI PC refresh cycle, maintaining cost discipline, and returning value to shareholders through dividends and buybacks. This article suggests that while considered a "boring" stock by some, HP Inc. offers stable cash flow and exposure to emerging tech trends, making it potentially appealing for diverse investors.

feed.title: Wabtec (NYSE: WAB) EVP uses 478 shares to settle tax withholding
feed.summary: Nicole B. Theophilus, EVP & Chief Admin. Officer at Wabtec (NYSE: WAB), disposed of 478 shares of common stock at $263.015 per share on March 2, 2026, to cover withholding taxes on vesting restricted shares. This transaction was a tax-withholding disposition, not an open-market sale, and she now directly holds 18,802 shares. The filing indicates a moderate impact and neutral sentiment.

feed.title: Medtronic, GE HealthCare Grow Strategic Partnership
feed.summary: Medtronic and GE HealthCare have announced a multi-year renewal and significant expansion of their strategic alliance, which has spanned over three decades. The expanded agreement aims to accelerate clinical and technological innovation, enabling healthcare providers to deliver smart, safe, and efficient patient care in hospitals. This collaboration will integrate Medtronic's advanced monitoring technologies with GE HealthCare's platforms and explore future innovations, including wireless wearable solutions and anesthesia airway visualization.

feed.title: Former J&J MedTech EMEA chair named CEO at Coloplast
feed.summary: Coloplast has appointed Gavin Wood, formerly the company group chair of Johnson & Johnson MedTech EMEA, as its new president and CEO, effective May 1, 2026. Wood brings two decades of leadership experience from Johnson & Johnson, including overseeing a multi-billion-dollar business and leading the Ethicon Wound Closure & Healing team, which aligns with Coloplast's wound care offerings. He will relocate to Denmark for his new role, with the board expressing confidence in his ability to drive growth and value creation.

feed.title: [Form 4] WESTINGHOUSE AIR BRAKE TECHNOLOGIES CORP Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Nalin Jain, President of Digital Electronics at WESTINGHOUSE AIR BRAKE TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WAB), reported a tax-related disposition of shares. On March 2, 2026, 1,101 shares of common stock were withheld by the company at $263.015 per share to cover tax obligations related to the vesting of restricted shares. Following this transaction, Jain directly owns 5,263 common shares.

feed.title: Let me see you One2PrEP: Gilead campaign targets stigma with pop culture punch
feed.summary: Gilead Sciences has launched its "One2PrEP" campaign, featuring a remix of Ciara's "1, 2 Step," to promote its new twice-yearly injectable PrEP, Yeztugo (lenacapavir). The campaign aims to destigmatize HIV prevention and increase PrEP uptake among the 2.2 million eligible individuals in the U.S. by making sexual health more normalized. Gilead emphasizes the importance of reaching communities most affected by HIV, including women, Black people, and Latino communities, to improve adherence and reduce HIV incidence.

feed.title: A Glimpse Into The Expert Outlook On Mid-America Apartment Through 12 Analysts
feed.summary: Twelve analysts have provided updated ratings for Mid-America Apartment (NYSE: MAA) over the past quarter, with an average price target of $146.42. The company's financial performance shows a strong market presence due to its market capitalization, but it faces challenges in profitability, return on equity, return on assets, and revenue growth compared to industry averages. Despite these financial hurdles, Mid-America Apartment maintains a sound financial structure with a debt-to-equity ratio below industry norms.

feed.title: Ford posts sales declines as EV, hybrid business continue to suffer
feed.summary: Ford Motor Company reported a 5.5% decline in February sales compared to the previous year, with a significant 71% drop in EV sales and a 22% dip in hybrid sales. The company's struggles in the EV sector are attributed to low demand and the elimination of tax credits, leading Ford to project continued losses on EVs until 2029. Despite these challenges, Ford plans to invest $2 billion to retool its Louisville Assembly Plant for all-electric pickup truck production by 2027.

feed.title: Micron: Why the Recent Pullback is an Opportunity
feed.summary: Micron Technology (MU) is positioned to benefit significantly from the AI boom due to its high-bandwidth memory (HBM) solutions, which are crucial for AI, HPC, and large-scale analytics. The company's HBM capacity for 2026 is already sold out, indicating strong revenue visibility and projected triple-digit earnings growth. Micron is also strengthening its AI partnerships with major players like NVIDIA, AMD, and Intel, further solidifying its market position and ensuring stable revenue streams.

feed.title: Wells Fargo FiNet Snags $1.7B UBS Team
feed.summary: A UBS advisor team, Snow Pine Private Wealth, managing over $1.7 billion in client assets, has moved to Wells Fargo Advisors Financial Network (FiNet). This move continues Wells Fargo's successful recruiting efforts, following a $6.3 billion team in December and two Citigroup advisors earlier in the year. Meanwhile, UBS has recently experienced significant advisor attrition, which it attributes to compensation changes aimed at improving profitability.

feed.title: Gap Stock With Contrarian Potential Ahead of Earnings
feed.summary: Gap Inc. (NYSE:GAP) is set to report its fourth-quarter earnings soon, with options traders showing bearish sentiment despite the stock's historically positive post-earnings performance. Although Gap stock is up significantly over the last year, a high put/call ratio and substantial short interest suggest that an unwinding of these bearish positions could provide a tailwind for the shares.

feed.title: Fomento Economico Mexicano, S.A.B. de C.V. (FMX) Reports Q4 Revenue Up 5.7% YoY
feed.summary: Fomento Económico Mexicano, S.A.B. de C.V. (FMX) reported a Q4 revenue increase of 5.7% year over year (YoY). The company's CEO, Jose Antonio Fernandez Garza-Laguera, highlighted strong performance from OXXO and Coca-Cola FEMSA, with OXXO traffic recovering and Coca-Cola FEMSA achieving consolidated volume growth. FMX also announced a restructuring effort to streamline operations and is optimistic about future growth despite newly implemented taxes in Mexico.

feed.title: Fidelity International High Dividend ETF (NYSE:FIDI) - Share Price and Research
feed.summary: This article provides current share price and historical data for Fidelity International High Dividend ETF (NYSE:FIDI). As of March 4, 2026, FIDI's share price is $27.76, reflecting a 2.60% drop from the previous close. The ETF recorded a 52-week high of $28.84 and a 52-week low of $19.44, with its last dividend payment of $0.25 on December 23, 2025.

feed.title: SA Quant's bottom manufacturing picks as factories shed jobs in Feb (FWDI:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: The article discusses the mixed signals for U.S. manufacturing, with private sector hiring seeing a modest pickup in February but the manufacturing sector continuing to shed jobs. Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings differentiate between manufacturing stocks, highlighting those with "Strong Sell" ratings based on various financial metrics. Forward Industries (FWDI:NASDAQ) and Lucid Group (LCID) are identified as having the lowest Quant Ratings among manufacturing stocks.

feed.title: Franklin FTSE India ETF (NYSE: FLIN) - Share Price
feed.summary: This article provides details on the current and historical share price performance of the Franklin FTSE India ETF (NYSE: FLIN). As of March 4, 2026, FLIN's share price is $35.84, representing a -1.48% decrease from its opening price. The report includes performance metrics for the current year, last year, and financial years, along with a daily share price history table.

feed.title: FDFF Stock Fund Price and Chart — NASDAQ:FDFF
feed.summary: This article provides detailed information about the Fidelity Disruptive Finance ETF (FDFF), including its current price, NAV, AUM, and performance metrics. It also outlines the fund's investment strategy, which focuses on long-term capital appreciation through companies involved in disruptive finance technologies like blockchain, digital payments, and AI-enabled underwriting. The article includes key stats, an overview of the issuer, and answers to frequently asked questions about ETFs and FDFF specifically.

feed.title: FEP ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns NASDAQ:FEP
feed.summary: The article provides an in-depth analysis of the First Trust Europe AlphaDEX Fund (FEP), an ETF designed to outperform the European market using a quantitative strategy. It details the fund's key statistics, including its $510.71 million AUM, 3.09% dividend yield, and 0.80% expense ratio. The analysis also covers FEP's sector and regional exposures, top holdings, dividend history, and recent performance metrics.

feed.title: Technical Analysis of Fidelity MSCI Consumer Discretionary Index ETF (AMEX:FDIS)
feed.summary: This article provides a technical analysis of the Fidelity MSCI Consumer Discretionary Index ETF (AMEX:FDIS), summarizing various indicators for different timeframes. It details oscillator and moving average readings, along with pivot points, to offer a comprehensive technical overview. The analysis concludes with a "sell" recommendation based on current technical ratings, while emphasizing that this information is not investment advice and market conditions are constantly changing.

feed.title: Shares withheld for taxes at Westinghouse Air Brake (NYSE: WAB)
feed.summary: Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies Corp executive David L. DeNinno had 2,243 shares of common stock withheld by the company to cover tax obligations related to the vesting of restricted shares. The shares were valued at $263.015 each. After this transaction, DeNinno directly owns 56,865 shares of common stock.

feed.title: EP/PC Stock Price and Chart — NYSE:EP/PC
feed.summary: This article provides detailed financial information and technical analysis for El Paso Corporation Preferred Stock (EP/PC) traded on the NYSE. It includes current stock price, historical performance data, key financial stats like dividend yield, and technical indicators for trading decisions. The piece also answers frequently asked questions about the stock's performance, volatility, and how to trade it.

feed.title: OGE fights for CWIP charges at power plant
feed.summary: Oklahoma Gas and Electric (OG&E) is engaged in a dispute with state organizations over its request to apply the new Construction Work in Progress (CWIP) law for its Horseshoe Lake Units 13 & 14 natural gas generation project. Opponents argue that OG&E's application, filed before the law's effective date, should not qualify, and that customers shouldn't pay for the project before completion. The Oklahoma Corporation Commissioners are set to deliberate on motions to dismiss OG&E's application.

feed.title: David Simon converts 20,619 RSUs to stock at Simon Property (NYSE: SPG)
feed.summary: David Simon, CEO, Chairman, and President of Simon Property Group (NYSE: SPG), settled 20,619 restricted stock units (RSUs) into common shares on March 2, 2026. This conversion resulted in Simon directly owning 1,071,453 shares of the company's common stock at a stated price of $0.00 per share, reflecting equity compensation rather than a cash transaction. The Form 4 filing details this transaction and his subsequent holdings.

feed.title: RTX Seeks Larger Role in FAA’s $12.5B ATC Overhaul
feed.summary: RTX is actively positioning its AutoTrac platform for the FAA's Brand New Air Traffic Control System (BNATCS) Common Automation Platform (CAP) contract, a major part of the $12.5 billion federal overhaul. The CAP aims to integrate the FAA's existing fragmented en route and terminal air traffic control systems into a single digital interface for improved efficiency and managing increasing air traffic. Given its current role as the primary contractor for the FAA's Standard Terminal Automation Replacement System (STARS) and global experience, RTX is well-suited for this significant contract.

feed.title: Van Den Berg Boosts MarketAxess Stake as Electronic Platforms Compete for Credit Trading Flow
feed.summary: Van Den Berg Management increased its stake in MarketAxess by acquiring an additional 16,382 shares, bringing its total holdings to $5.51 million. This transaction highlights the ongoing competition among electronic platforms in the credit trading market, where MarketAxess is a key player in U.S. corporate credit. Investors are evaluating MarketAxess's ability to maintain its market share as electronic bond trading evolves.

feed.title: What Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM)'s Earnings Beat Amid Accounting Probe Means For Shareholders
feed.summary: Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) reported stronger-than-expected adjusted earnings in Q4 2025 despite an ongoing accounting probe and leadership changes. This earnings beat highlights the company's resilience as it transitions towards higher-margin food innovation and bio-based products, leveraging its traditional grain and oilseed network. While the results support ADM's strategic shift, investors are closely watching for clarity on biofuels policy and resolution of internal control and regulatory scrutiny.

feed.title: Duke Energy Florida Reliability Gains Put Grid Investment Story In Focus
authors: Simply Wall St Reviewed by Bailey Pemberton
feed.summary: Duke Energy Florida, a part of Duke Energy (NYSE:DUK), has achieved its lowest customer outage duration in over two decades, attributing this success to significant grid hardening investments. This milestone, driven by stronger poles, underground lines, and smart grid technology, underscores the company's commitment to reliability and resilience in a state prone to severe weather. For investors, these operational improvements are crucial as they can impact future rate cases, customer satisfaction, and the overall financial health and long-term positioning of Duke Energy.

feed.title: Popular Chain Restaurant Closing Most Remaining Locations In Coming Weeks
feed.summary: Darden Restaurants Inc. announced that it will close 14 Bahama Breeze locations by April 5, 2026, and convert the remaining 14 locations into other Darden brands over the next 12-18 months. The company does not expect these actions to significantly impact its financial results. The article lists the specific locations slated for closure and conversion, with a notable concentration in Florida.

feed.title: CoStar Group insiders, including CEO, buy shares after stock slides (CSGP:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Insiders at CoStar Group (CSGP), including the CEO, President of Marketplaces, and a director, have purchased a total of 76,720 shares. These purchases occurred after the company's stock experienced a 28% decline year-to-date. CEO Andrew Florance alone acquired 55,720 shares.

feed.title: Broadridge Pass Through Voting Upgrade Sparks Interest In Undervalued Shares
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions (NYSE:BR) has launched a significant Pass-Through Voting upgrade for its ProxyVote platform, aimed at simplifying how individual investors vote on fund holdings. Vanguard is the first asset manager to adopt this feature, which is expected to extend to millions of investors as more managers integrate it. This initiative could give individual investors greater influence over corporate governance topics like board elections and ESG proposals, routed through their fund holdings, boosting Broadridge's central role in proxy infrastructure.

feed.title: The Walt Disney Company Secures up to $10 Billion in Loans Ahead of New CEO Takeover
feed.summary: The Walt Disney Company has secured two new credit lines totaling almost $10 billion, weeks before Josh D’Amaro takes over as CEO. These unsecured credit agreements, a $5.25 billion short-term line and a $4 billion long-term line, replace expiring ones and are intended for general operating costs. Notably, these funds cannot be used for FuboTV or specific international theme parks like Shanghai and Hong Kong Disneyland.

feed.title: Popular Chain Restaurant Closing Most Remaining Locations In Coming Weeks
feed.summary: Bahama Breeze, a popular Caribbean-inspired chain restaurant owned by Darden Restaurants Inc., is closing 14 of its locations by April 5, 2026. The remaining 14 locations will be converted into other Darden brands over the next 12-18 months. Darden does not expect these closures and conversions to significantly impact its financial results.
